title: Where You Go, I'll Follow
epigraph: Whither thou goest, I will go; thy people shall be my people.
styleNote: Cinematic Folk Vow · Soft Piano to Grand Swell · Female Lead Vocal
listenLine: Press play and walk the road of Ruth’s vow.

sections.label: Intro
lines: Through the fields of sorrow,
dust upon my feet,
Every step beside you,
love that won’t retreat.
The road is long, the nights grow cold,
But hand in hand, we’re brave and bold.

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: Your voice a whisper in the wind,
Calling me to start again.
No past to hold, no chains remain,
Just love that speaks through loss and pain.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: I’ve left behind the life I knew,
For something deeper, something true.
No turning back, no parting ways,
In every storm, I choose to stay.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: Where you go, I will follow,
Where you stay, I will stay.
Your people will be my people,
Your God will be my God.
Where you go, I will follow,
Even unto the grave.
Nothing but death will part us,
Forever, my love remains.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: Through the golden fields we walk,
Hope reborn in every thought.
A promise stitched in threads of fate,
Love that even time won’t break.
I’ll be your shadow in the light.
No force could tear this vow apart,
For love is written in my heart.

sections.label: Chorus 2
lines: Where you go, I will follow,
Where you stay, I will stay.
Your people will be my people,
and God, my God.
Your heart will never be alone.
Where you go, I will follow,
Even unto the grave.
Nothing but death will part us,
Forever, my love remains.

sections.label: Outro
lines: And when the stars fade from the sky,
Still by your side, I’ll close my eyes.
A love unbroken, sealed by grace,

sections.label: Final Chorus
lines: Where you go, I will follow,
Where you stay, I will stay.
Your people will be my people,
and your God will be mine.
Where you go, I will follow,
Even unto the grave.
Nothing but death will part us,
Forever, my love remains.

shareText: Where You Go, I'll Follow — a cinematic folk vow from the Book of Ruth, by Selah Sound Collective.
